skill.name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter-py
skill.description: Guidance for configuring the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter for Python with Application Insights traces, metrics, logs, sampling, authentication, and storage options.
skill.summary: Configure Python OpenTelemetry exporters for Azure Monitor and Application Insights.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown examples, inline identifiers, and table text rather than executable skill code. The only confirmed risk factor is expected Azure Monitor telemetry configuration with placeholder ingestion endpoints, so the skill is suitable to publish with low-risk context.

low_findings.title: Azure Telemetry Endpoint Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes placeholder Application Insights ingestion endpoint URLs. This is expected for Azure Monitor exporter guidance and does not show unauthorized exfiltration, but users should understand that configured exporters send telemetry to Azure Monitor.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are visible placeholder Azure Application Insights ingestion endpoints. Context shows they are configuration examples for the documented telemetry exporter, not hidden network behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misidentified as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many backticked Markdown snippets as Ruby or shell execution. Review found documentation, code fences, inline package names, and Python examples in SKILL.md, with no executable script or command dispatch logic in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown code fences, inline identifiers, tables, and example Python configuration. No file contains runnable skill code that invokes a shell or Ruby backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations do not contain a cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation. They are frontmatter description text and a configuration table header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or comparable weak cryptographic usage at the flagged locations. The hit appears to be a scanner pattern mismatch.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: Backtick and fenced-code syntax appears in documentation examples only. No external command execution behavior was found in the skill file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Every flagged external-command location is in Markdown or example content. The repository contains only SKILL.md according to the report file structure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network URL Pattern Is Expected Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The hardcoded URLs are placeholder Azure Monitor ingestion endpoints used to show connection string formats. They are not contacted by the skill itself.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are in environment-variable and sovereign-cloud configuration examples. The skill provides guidance and contains no runtime networking code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Crypto Pattern Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak-cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic calls, algorithms, or key handling. No evidence of weak cryptography was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive frontmatter and a table heading. The semantic context does not include cryptographic implementation.

content.value_statement: Custom Python telemetry pipelines need clear exporter setup for traces, metrics, and logs. This skill gives focused Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry patterns for Application Insights integration.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry exporter package for Python.
Provides setup patterns for trace, metric, and log exporters.
Explains connection string use through direct configuration or environment variables.
Covers Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential.
Documents sampling, offline storage, sovereign cloud endpoints, and exporter type mapping.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run or validate telemetry code.
It assumes the project already uses Python and OpenTelemetry concepts.
It focuses on the low-level exporter, not the full Azure Monitor distro.
It does not provide production monitoring architecture or cost guidance.

use_cases.title: Add Application Insights tracing
use_cases.description: Configure a Python service to export spans to Azure Monitor with a batch span processor.

use_cases.title: Build custom telemetry pipelines
use_cases.description: Choose low-level exporters when automatic instrumentation is not enough for a service design.

use_cases.title: Prepare Azure production settings
use_cases.description: Review authentication, sampling, offline storage, and sovereign cloud configuration options.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the right package
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide whether to use azure-monitor-opentelemetry or azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter for a Python service.

prompt_templates.title: Configure trace export
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to configure AzureMonitorTraceExporter with BatchSpanProcessor for a Python web service.

prompt_templates.title: Add metrics and logs
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through adding Azure Monitor metric and log exporters to an existing OpenTelemetry Python setup.

prompt_templates.title: Design production exporter settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry exporter plan for authentication, sampling, offline storage, and sovereign cloud support.

output_examples.input: I need traces from a Python service in Application Insights.
output: Use the low-level trace exporter when you already control the OpenTelemetry pipeline.
Configure a tracer provider, attach a batch span processor, and use an Application Insights connection string or environment variable.
Prefer Azure AD authentication for production where supported.

output_examples.input: I need reliable telemetry export during temporary network failures.
output: Keep offline storage enabled unless the environment forbids local retry storage.
Set a storage directory that matches the service runtime permissions.
Use sampling to control traffic volume before data reaches Azure Monitor.

output_examples.input: I run in Azure Government and need exporter settings.
output: Use the appropriate Azure authority host for credentials.
Set the Application Insights ingestion endpoint for the sovereign cloud.
Confirm that identity, endpoint, and resource configuration all use the same cloud boundary.

best_practices: Use BatchSpanProcessor for production trace export.
Use Azure AD authentication instead of long-lived instrumentation keys when possible.
Set sampling and export intervals based on service traffic and monitoring needs.
anti_patterns: Do not use the low-level exporter when the Azure Monitor distro already meets your needs.
Do not hardcode production connection strings in source files.
Do not disable offline storage without an explicit reliability or compliance reason.

faq.question: What does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the Python Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry exporter for traces, metrics, logs, authentication, sampling, storage, and endpoints.

faq.question: Is this the same as the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry distro?
faq.answer: No. This focuses on the low-level exporter for custom OpenTelemetry pipelines.

faq.question: Can it generate a full observability architecture?
faq.answer: No. It provides exporter configuration guidance, not a complete monitoring platform design.

faq.question: Does it support Application Insights?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples target Application Insights through Azure Monitor exporter configuration.

faq.question: Should I use instrumentation keys or Azure AD?
faq.answer: Use Azure AD authentication when possible. Use connection strings only when they fit your environment and policy.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It contains documentation and example snippets. Users decide whether to run installation or application code.
